items.text: Hi Sam, you're signed up for the Friday campus tour at 2:00 PM, meeting near Anderson Hall. Please wear the blue guide shirt and bring your $5 refundable deposit for the radio earpiece. See you then. — Ms. Chen, Visitor Center

questions.stem: According to the email, where should Sam meet for the tour?
questions.explanation: The email states the meeting place is near Anderson Hall at 2:00 PM.

questions.stem: What must Sam bring on Friday?
questions.explanation: The email asks Sam to bring a $5 refundable deposit for the earpiece.

items.text: Campus Photography Contest! Submit your best shot of student life by March 22 at noon. Winner receives a $150 gift card. Judges: Prof. Park and the Art Department staff. Drop entries at the Student Union front desk. Open to all undergraduates only.

questions.stem: According to the flyer, what is the submission deadline?
questions.explanation: The flyer states submissions are due by March 22 at noon.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about graduate students?

options.A: They will judge the entries this year
options.B: They are not eligible for the contest
options.C: They receive a smaller cash prize
options.D: They must submit photos in person

questions.explanation: Because the flyer says the contest is open only to undergraduates, graduate students cannot enter.

items.text: Tried the new lavender oat latte at Birch Café this morning — actually great! It's $4.50 with a student ID and they're only serving it through April 30. Honestly, get one before midterms start. Open weekdays 7 AM–6 PM in Holden Hall.

questions.stem: According to the post, what is required to get the $4.50 price?
questions.explanation: The post says the drink is $4.50 with a student ID.

questions.stem: What can be inferred from the post?

options.A: Birch Café opens before classes begin
options.B: The drink is sold all year at this price
options.C: Holden Hall is closed on weekends
options.D: The drink will be discontinued after April

questions.explanation: Because the drink is served only through April 30, it can be inferred that it will be discontinued after April.

items.text: Dear residents, any meal plan balance over $20 will be refunded to your student account after May 18. Smaller amounts are not eligible. Stop by the Dining Office in Pearson Hall by Friday to confirm your mailing address. — Dining Services

questions.stem: According to the email, what condition must a balance meet to qualify for a refund?

options.B: It must be on a meal plan card
options.C: It must be confirmed in person
options.D: It must be mailed before May 18

questions.explanation: The email says balances over $20 will be refunded; smaller amounts are not eligible.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about students with smaller balances?

options.A: They will receive a partial credit later
options.B: They can mail a written appeal to staff
options.C: They will hear from Dining Services again
options.D: They will not receive any money back

questions.explanation: Because smaller amounts are not eligible for a refund, those students will not receive any money back.
